title: The Cursed Sword Test
description: On pages 9–12, the cursed sword test focuses on Zoro, Tashigi, Nami, Luffy as the crew reaches Loguetown, where Zoro searches for swords and runs into Tashigi.
scene: On pages 9–12, the cursed sword test focuses on Zoro, Tashigi, Nami, Luffy as the crew reaches Loguetown, where Zoro searches for swords and runs into Tashigi.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: So you love swords, do you?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: If you don't mind me saying so, your 3 swords remind me of a certain bounty hunter!

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: A certain bounty hunter...?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: I'm talking of Roronoa Zoro. Do you know him?

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: I have heard of his name quite a bit.

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: Yes, he's quite a famous swordsman here in East Blue.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Or should I say, infamous?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: to think that he treats his swords as merely a means of making money...

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: It's simply UNFOR- GIVABLE!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Why is it that the evil are so strong in this day and age...?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: All the famous swordsmen of today are either pirates or bounty hunters.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: And they're the ones who possess most of the world's masterwork blades.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: The swords are crying, you know.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Well, perhaps they all have their own special circumstances, no?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: occu- Pations reflect the needs of the time, after all.

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: I'd be more than happy to welcome any criminals!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I remember when my shop used to be teeming with all sorts of people trying to enter the grand line.

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: But ever since that monster was placed in charge of this town,

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: I barely get any customers anymore!

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: S-Smoker is no monster!

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: He's a devil fruit! User! That's more than enough reason to call him a monster!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Devil fruit...?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: In any case... being a swordsman myself,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I vow to train harder and harder with my shiqure,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Such that I can travel the world and retrieve all the masterwork blades from the hands of criminals!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Whether it be the 12 supreme- grade swords, 21 great-grade swords, or the 50 skillful-grade swords, I shall risk my life to retrieve them!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Does that mean you'll take this sword too?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: This sword you call Wadou Ichimonji...

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: What!?

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: Oh, no, not at all. It's not that I want these swords for myself.

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: I simply detest the idea of them being used for evil.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Hm...

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: AH!? That sword is...!

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: I remember seeing this in my book before!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: SANDAI KITETSU!

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: You should definitely buy this sword!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Mister!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Are you really selling this sword for only 50,000!?

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: Y-Yes...

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: That's amazing! This is another high-grade sword!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: It should normally be sold for a million belli.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: It's predecessor "Nidal Kitetsu" is one of the great-grade swords,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: While "shodai Kitetsu" is one of the supreme- grade swords!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: No, I changed my mind! I can't sell that sword!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: What!? But why!?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: It's cursed, right?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: You knew...?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: No...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I could tell.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: ...it's as you said.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Beginning with the shodai Kitetsu, the Kitetsu line of blades are renowned for their quality, but they're also cursed swords!

summary: This part gives Zoro a major equipment and character moment while Loguetown’s Marines begin to matter.
